sections.paragraph:
ἀγωνιάω impf. ἠγωνίων; 1 aor. ptc. ἀγωνιάσας (Esth 5:1e); inf. ἀγωνιᾶσαι (Just., A I, 4, 2) be anxious, be distressed (so in later writers, including Dio Chrys. 4, 55 [w. μή foll.]; ins, pap [s. Preis.; Witkowski index], LXX; Jos., Ant. 9, 32) ἀγωνιῶντες μεγάλως (PGiss 19, 3; 2 Macc 3:21; cp. συναγωνιάω ‘be in fearful suspense’ Polyb. 3, 43, 7 and 8) full of great anxiety GPt 11:45; w. indir. quest. foll. μήποτε (UPZ 62, 30 [161/160 b.c.]; w. μή Just., A I, 4, 2) 5:15. W. gen. abs. foll. οὐ γὰρ μικρῶς ἀγωνιῶ τοιούτου σου ὄντος I am quite worried seeing you like this (in reaction to the disturbed countenance of Jesus) AcPl Ha 7, 31.—DELG s.v. ἄγω.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

Witkowski Witkowski = SW., Epistulae Privatae Graecae—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

indir. indir. = indirect

quest. quest. = question

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

gen. gen. = genitive

abs. abs. = absolute

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 17
